growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (high light, 30 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4 Effect of light intensity on growth of R. palustris CGA009 wild type and pucBA mutants. Wild type: High light 10 (0.4) h; Low light 20 (3) h; Extremely low light 185 (4) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (10 h) reported in Table 4 for CGA009 wild type under high light (30 µmol photons/m2/s), anaerobic photoheterotrophic growth on PM mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract, 30°C. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/10 = 0.0693 h-1.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (low light, 4 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: Wild type doubling time under Low light (4 µmol photons/m2/s) = 20 (3) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (20 h) reported in Table 4 for CGA009 wild type under low light, same medium/conditions as high-light entry. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/20 = 0.0347 h-1.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (extremely low light, <1 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: Wild type doubling time under Extremely low light (<1 µmol photons/m2/s) = 185 (4) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (185 h) reported in Table 4 for CGA009 wild type under extremely low light. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/185 = 0.00375 h-1.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (extremely low light, <1 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: ΔpucBAa/ΔLH2A doubling time Extremely low light = 195 (3) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (195 h) for LH2A-deletion mutant under extremely low light. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/195 = 0.00355 h-1. Slightly slower than WT (185h), indicating LH2A dispensable but marginal role at ELL.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (low light, 4 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: ΔpucBAd/ΔLH4 doubling time Low light = 22 (2) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (22 h) for LH4-deletion mutant under low light. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/22 = 0.0315 h-1.

growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (extremely low light, <1 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: ΔpucBAd/ΔLH4 doubling time Extremely low light = 275 (26) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (275 h) for LH4-deletion mutant under extremely low light; markedly slower than WT (185h), consistent with LH4 being required at ELL. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/275 = 0.00252 h-1.

growth_records.strain: CGA009 ΔpucBAa ΔpucBAd (ΔLH2A ΔLH4 double mutant)
growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (low light, 4 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: ΔpucBAa ΔpucBAd/ΔLH2A LH4 doubling time Low light = 29 (5) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (29 h) for double mutant under low light; slower than either single mutant, confirming LH2A can compensate for LH4 loss at LL. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/29 = 0.0239 h-1.

growth_records.strain: CGA009 ΔpucBAa ΔpucBAd (ΔLH2A ΔLH4 double mutant)
growth_records.medium: PM defined mineral medium + 20 mM acetate + 0.1% yeast extract (extremely low light, <1 µmol photons/m2/s)
growth_records.cite: Fixen et al. Clades of Photosynthetic Bacteria Belonging to the Genus Rhodopseudomonas Show Marked Diversity in Light-Harvesting Antenna Complex Gene Composition and Expression. mSystems (2015). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00006-15. [PMC5069747]
growth_records.snippet: TABLE 4: ΔpucBAa ΔpucBAd/ΔLH2A LH4 doubling time Extremely low light = 365 (43) h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time (365 h) for double mutant under extremely low light, slowest of all genotypes/conditions tested. growth_rate_per_h = ln(2)/365 = 0.0019 h-1.

growth_records.strain: recombinant R. palustris-pCE2TA-nao1 / -nao2 (carbon-fixing host carrying narGHI cluster, expressing heterologous naoABC)
growth_records.medium: MSM (mineral salts medium) + NaH13CO3, autotrophic denitrifying

up.compound: Sb(III) (antimony, electron donor; stoichiometric with Sb(V) produced)
up.method: AFS (HPLC-atomic fluorescence); derived from Sb(V) appearance under IPTG induction

up.compound: CO2 / bicarbonate (autotrophic carbon source, 10 mM NaH13CO3)
up.units: not quantified (13C incorporation into biomass confirmed by IRMS/nanoSIMS but no uptake rate reported)

sec.method: AFS; IPTG-induced increase from 1.25-1.75 to 21.75-23.24 uM over 8 d

growth_records.cite: Wang, Liying et al. Nitrate-dependent antimony oxidase in an uncultured Symbiobacteriaceae member. The ISME Journal (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrae204. [PMC11521347]
growth_records.snippet: the overexpressed Nao proteins significantly increased the dissolved Sb(V) from 1.25-1.75 to 21.75-23.24 uM, and increased cell numbers from 4.20x10^5-5.86x10^5 to 4.59x10^6-4.76x10^6 cells/mL; cell numbers (from 1.04x10^5 to 4.20x10^5 for
growth_records.notes: GEOCHEMICAL/AUTOTROPHIC REDOX system, not classical heterotrophic GEM physiology; included as a defined pure-culture prokaryote growth condition with measured substrate transformation. Growth_rate CALCULATED from explicit Nao1 (no-IPTG) cell counts: mu = ln(4.20e5/1.04e5)/(8 d x 24 h) = ln(4.038)/192 = 1.396/192 = 0.00727 /h; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.00727 = 95.3 h. Nao2 (no-IPTG): 1.01e5->5.86e5 -> mu = ln(5.802)/192 = 1.758/192 = 0.00916 /h, td = 75.7 h. IPTG-induced (faster): ~1.0e5->~4.68e6 (mid of 4.59-4.76e6) over 8 d -> mu = ln(46.8)/192 = 3.846/192 = 0.0200 /h, td = 34.6 h. Sb secretion (IPTG): net Sb(V) ~ (22.5-1.5) = 21 uM over 8 d = 2.63 uM/day = 21e-3 mmol/L / 192 h = 1.09e-4 mmol/L/h; Sb(III) uptake ~ equal (stoichiometric oxidation). Full MSM composition is in Supplementary Information (not in main text) -> medium_composition_stated:false; only amended components listed. MIC of Sb(III) for R. palustris in MSM = 0.5 mM; 0.1 mM had no metabolic effect (why 0.1 mM chosen). No biomass in gDW -> rates volumetric only, cannot convert to mmol/gDW/h. Carbon fixed from 13C-bicarbonate (autotrophy) confirmed by IRMS + nanoSIMS but CO2 uptake rate not reported. Control R. palustris-pCE2TA (no nao) showed no Sb(III) oxidation and no 13C incorporation above control.

up.method: 13C-MFA flux measurement (experimental), cited from McKinlay & Harwood 2010 PNAS 107:11669 [ref 48]; used to constrain the GSM

sec.method: Measured CO2 export (experimental), cited from McKinlay & Harwood 2010 PNAS 107:11669 [ref 48]; equals ~22% of acetate uptake flux

mfa.note: Experimental 13C-MFA snapshot of photoheterotrophic acetate metabolism (McKinlay & Harwood 2010, ref 48) used to validate/constrain the iAN1128 GSM
Measured CO2 export flux (ref 48); ~22% of imported acetate carbon released as CO2

growth_records.cite: Navid, Ali et al. System-level analysis of metabolic trade-offs during anaerobic photoheterotrophic growth in Rhodopseudomonas palustris. BMC Bioinformatics (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12859-019-2844-z. [PMC6509789]
growth_records.snippet: At a fixed uptake rate of acetate that matches experimental flux measurements (1.96 mmol.gDW-1.h-1)... we set the rate of CO2 export equal to the measured value (0.23 mmol.gDW-1.h-1)... predicted doubling time of 6.4 h vs. measured 8.4 +/-
growth_records.notes: R. palustris CGA009, anaerobic photoheterotrophic batch on acetate 10 mM in modified photosynthetic medium (phosphate lowered to 5 mM, initial pH 6.7), 60 W incandescent light at 20 cm; this study grew the cultures (OD660) and measured spent-media pH (Fig 3). Experimental acetate uptake 1.96 mmol/gDW/h and CO2 export 0.23 mmol/gDW/h are cited from McKinlay & Harwood 2010 PNAS 107:11669-11675 (ref 48). Measured doubling time 8.4 +/- 0.6 h cited from McKinlay & Harwood [ref 51]. mu = ln2/td = 0.693/8.4 = 0.0825 /h (CALCULATED from cited td). Theoretical max H2 yield 4 mol H2/mol acetate (model, stated to match prior experimental value). Temperature not stated.

up.method: MODEL-predicted substrate import rate (MOFA/FBA), not experimental (Table 1)

sec.method: Model-predicted 3% of imported benzoate carbon exported as CO2 (Table 1); ~90% of produced CO2 fixed into biomass

growth_records.cite: Navid, Ali et al. System-level analysis of metabolic trade-offs during anaerobic photoheterotrophic growth in Rhodopseudomonas palustris. BMC Bioinformatics (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12859-019-2844-z. [PMC6509789]
growth_records.snippet: Benzoate ... Measured doubling time 9.3 +/- 0.2 h [62] ... Rate of substrate import 0.43 mmol/gDW.h ... Percent of imported carbon as CO2 = 3
growth_records.notes: R. palustris CGA009 anaerobic photoheterotrophic batch on benzoate 3 mM (modified photosynthetic medium, 5 mM phosphate, initial pH 6.7, 60 W light at 20 cm). Measured doubling time 9.3 +/- 0.2 h cited from ref 62 (Table 1). mu = ln2/td = 0.693/9.3 = 0.0745 /h (CALCULATED from cited td). Substrate import rate 0.43 mmol/gDW/h is MODEL-predicted (is_experimental false). CO2 export 3% of imported carbon is model-predicted. Temperature not stated.

up.method: MODEL-predicted substrate import rate (MOFA/FBA), not experimental (Table 1)

sec.method: Model-predicted 8% of imported 4HBZ carbon exported as CO2 (Table 1)

growth_records.cite: Navid, Ali et al. System-level analysis of metabolic trade-offs during anaerobic photoheterotrophic growth in Rhodopseudomonas palustris. BMC Bioinformatics (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12859-019-2844-z. [PMC6509789]
growth_records.snippet: 4HBZ ... Measured doubling time 12 +/- 0.2 h [62] ... Rate of substrate import 0.44 mmol/gDW.h ... Percent of imported carbon as CO2 = 8
growth_records.notes: R. palustris CGA009 anaerobic photoheterotrophic batch on 4-hydroxybenzoate 2.2 mM (modified photosynthetic medium, 5 mM phosphate, initial pH 6.7, 60 W light at 20 cm). Measured doubling time 12 +/- 0.2 h cited from ref 62 (Table 1). mu = ln2/td = 0.693/12 = 0.0578 /h (CALCULATED from cited td). Substrate import 0.44 mmol/gDW/h is MODEL-predicted (is_experimental false). CO2 export 8% of imported carbon model-predicted. Model growth for 4HBZ was the least accurate of the aromatics. Temperature not stated.

up.method: MODEL-predicted substrate import rate (MOFA/FBA), not experimental (Table 1)

sec.method: Model-predicted 4.6% of imported 4-coumarate carbon exported as CO2; ~83% of produced CO2 fixed into biomass (Table 1)

growth_records.cite: Navid, Ali et al. System-level analysis of metabolic trade-offs during anaerobic photoheterotrophic growth in Rhodopseudomonas palustris. BMC Bioinformatics (2019). DOI:10.1186/s12859-019-2844-z. [PMC6509789]
growth_records.snippet: 4-Coumarate ... Measured doubling time 9.4 +/- 0.2 h [62] ... Rate of substrate import 0.33 mmol/gDW.h ... Percent of imported carbon as CO2 = 4.6
growth_records.notes: R. palustris CGA009 anaerobic photoheterotrophic batch on 4-coumarate 2 mM (modified photosynthetic medium, 5 mM phosphate, initial pH 6.7, 60 W light at 20 cm). Measured doubling time 9.4 +/- 0.2 h cited from ref 62 (Table 1). mu = ln2/td = 0.693/9.4 = 0.0737 /h (CALCULATED from cited td). Substrate import 0.33 mmol/gDW/h is MODEL-predicted (is_experimental false). CO2 export 4.6% of imported carbon model-predicted. Temperature not stated.

growth_records.strain: CGA676 (NifA* mutant, constitutive NH4+ excretion under N2-fixing conditions)

up.method: 20 mM acetate as carbon/electron source (monoculture); qualitative
N2 fixed via nitrogenase (NifA* constitutive); qualitative

sec.method: excreted under N2-fixing conditions due to NifA* mutation; cross-fed to E. coli
nitrogenase byproduct; hoxJ/hupV genotype modulates H2 uptake/oxidation

growth_records.cite: Chuang, Ying-Chih et al. Reciprocal cross-feeding between bacteria can limit the emergence of metabolic dependencies. Applied and Environmental Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.1128/aem.00363-26. [PMC13274399]
growth_records.snippet: monocultures of phototrophic, N2-fixing, NH4+-excreting R. palustris... grown as monocultures (MDC with 20 mM acetate and 10 mM NH4Cl)... nifA* mutation that causes NH4+ excretion under N2-fixing conditions.
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic phototrophic N2-fixing R. palustris NifA* monoculture on defined MDC (acetate + NH4Cl stated). NH4+ and H2 excretion and N2/acetate uptake reported qualitatively; nitrogenase reaction 3H2? note: nitrogenase reduces N2 to 2 NH4+ and produces H2. Growth by OD660; numeric mu only in figure bar charts, left null. Experimental-evolution study; rates qualitative.
